statement.en: Nonlinear Stability Analysis of Stationary Solutions for A Special Class of Reaction-diﬀusion-convection Systems With Respect To Small Perturbation
statement.summary: We study the stability of multidimensional planar fronts for a reaction-diﬀusionconvection system introduced in combustion theory with respect to spatially localized perturbations. In the thesis, we investigate the nonlinear stability of the trivial solution with respect to a small perturbation. For the reaction-diﬀusion-convection system in multidimensional space, we employ exponentially weighted spaces to shift the spectrum of the linearized diﬀerential operator and determine the spectrum of the equations after adding the drift term. We analyze the nonlinear equations on the intersection of the unweighted and weighted space. We demonstrate that the unweighted norm of the equations is bounded by small initials and and that the weighted norm decays algebraically as time progresses.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely metadata error or provisional submission). The mathematical claims are internally structured and employ established PDE techniques (exponentially weighted spaces, spectral analysis, linearization), but categorization as 'provisional' reflects: (1) preprint status not yet peer-validated, (2) future timestamp suggesting incomplete review cycle, (3) nonlinear stability results require rigorous verification of decay estimates and spectral assumptions. The core theorems appear sound in methodology but await publication validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 小さな種子は留まり、重い霧の中でゆっくり消える。
